reforms.description: The government has initiated a major investment programme under the National Development Plan, allocating €698 million to enhance research and development in higher education institutions. This unprecedented increase in funding aims to strengthen research activities at universities, with allocations based on competitive evaluation by a third party. Additionally, the government is considering reintroducing third-level fees for full-time students from families that can afford to pay, along with government-backed student loans to improve access and generate income for the education system.
reforms.package_name: Higher Education and R&D Funding Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D and improved access to higher education are expected to enhance innovation capacity and human capital, supporting long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding programme is actively in place, and the government is moving forward with the proposed changes to higher education fees.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in funding and access to higher education, which is critical for enhancing research capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: The question of re-introducing third-level fees for those full-time students whose families can afford to pay is under consideration by the Department of Education and Science.

reforms.description: More systematic efforts will be needed to address the weaknesses in technology transfer and commercialisation of research results through improved cooperation between businesses and higher education institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing technology transfer is crucial for translating research into marketable innovations, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for improved cooperation has been recognized, and efforts are underway to enhance technology transfer mechanisms.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform focuses on improving existing processes rather than instituting a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: More systematic efforts will be needed to address the weaknesses in technology transfer and commercialisation of research results.

reforms.description: Ireland has embarked on the use of Public Private Partnerships (PPP) for priority infrastructure projects under the National Development Plan, aiming to achieve accelerated delivery of strategic national infrastructure and quality public services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Utilizing PPPs is expected to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of infrastructure delivery, which can support innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The PPP framework is actively being utilized for infrastructure projects as part of the National Development Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant shift in how infrastructure projects are financed and delivered, impacting the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Ireland has embarked on the use of Public Private Partnerships (PPP) for priority infrastructure projects under the National Development Plan.

reforms.description: Ireland's industrial policy is shifting focus from production to research and innovation activities to enhance its competitive edge in a globalized economy. This strategic change aims to foster clusters of excellence that integrate technology companies, education, and research activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Focusing on research and innovation is expected to improve competitiveness and drive economic growth through enhanced collaboration and cluster development.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategic shift in industrial policy has been articulated, with plans for implementation underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: This represents a fundamental change in industrial policy, emphasizing the importance of R&D and innovation for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Irish industrial policy is changing its focus away from production activities towards research and innovation activities in an attempt to move up the value chain.
